vulnerabilities.description: Hardcoded demo password 'platform123' in DEMO_USERS array - credentials visible in source code and git history
Hardcoded demo password 'admin123' in DEMO_USERS array - credentials visible in source code and git history
Hardcoded demo password 'staff123' in DEMO_USERS array - credentials visible in source code and git history
Hardcoded demo password 'owner123' in DEMO_USERS array - credentials visible in source code and git history
Hardcoded demo password 'resident123' in DEMO_USERS array - credentials visible in source code and git history
Hardcoded default password 'admin123' in login form state - exposed in browser code and source control
Authentication completely bypassed in demo mode - when Supabase is not configured, all requests pass through with synthetic principal despite warning log
Real email addresses and phone numbers in DEMO_USERS - leaked PII (admin@hellaspm.gr, staff@hellaspm.gr, 210-9993311, 210-9993312)
API key passed in fetch headers without additional validation - ANTHROPIC_API_KEY could be exposed in error logs, network monitoring, or monitoring tools if request fails
Error messages may expose sensitive API request details - could leak base64 image data or API errors in logs

Webhook endpoint at /api/webhooks/vivawallet is mounted BEFORE requireAuth middleware, allowing unauthenticated access - while it claims server-to-server verification, the webhook handling code is truncated and cannot be fully verified
CORS origin configured from environment variable (VITE_APP_URL) without validation - if not set, defaults to localhost which is permissive
Auth data stored in localStorage with predictable key ('hpm_auth_user') - vulnerable to XSS, but user data format appears reasonable
URL sanitization function is well-implemented with protocol whitelist (http/https only) - no vulnerability, but verify it's used on all user-supplied URLs

recommendations: CRITICAL: Remove all hardcoded passwords from DEMO_USERS array in src/lib/auth.ts immediately. Generate temporary credentials via environment variables or remove demo account credentials entirely from production builds
CRITICAL: Remove default password 'admin123' from LoginView.tsx state initialization - use empty string instead

CRITICAL: Force authentication requirement - replace demo mode bypass with early startup error if Supabase is not configured. Demo mode should never silently allow unauthenticated requests in a production-like environment
CRITICAL: Replace real email addresses and phone numbers in DEMO_USERS with generic placeholders (demo@example.com, 555-0000-0000)
HIGH: Add validation and logging for ANTHROPIC_API_KEY usage - never expose the actual key in error messages or logs. Sanitize error responses before sending to client
HIGH: Verify webhook signature validation in payments.ts webhook handler. Implement HMAC-SHA256 or similar verification using Viva's webhook secret
HIGH: Add rate limiting on OCR endpoint (/api/expenses/scan) to prevent abuse and cost overruns from malicious requests
MEDIUM: Validate VITE_APP_URL environment variable is a valid origin before using in CORS headers - reject if empty or invalid
MEDIUM: Implement request signing/verification for Anthropic API calls - use request/response validation beyond basic status checks
MEDIUM: Add comprehensive input validation on all API endpoints - validate request body structure, data types, and ranges
MEDIUM: Implement Content Security Policy (CSP) headers to mitigate XSS risks, especially given user-supplied URL handling
LOW: Add secret rotation policy for API keys - ensure .env files are never committed and .gitignore includes all credential files
LOW: Consider removing localStorage usage for sensitive auth data - use httpOnly cookies instead (if possible with current architecture)
LOW: Add security headers (X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, Strict-Transport-Security) to all responses
